official.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
official.office_address: 250 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3214

summary.narrative: On her core 2018 campaign agenda, Ocasio-Cortez has been a consistent and visible sponsor and advocate but has secured few enacted outcomes this term. Several commitments — universal background checks, the $15 minimum wage, and anti-discrimination protections — passed the House but died in the Senate, while signature items like Medicare for All, the Green New Deal, tuition-free college, student debt cancellation, and Hyde repeal remain at the advocacy or bill-introduction stage. The dominant pattern is sustained legislative effort and sponsorship constrained by a chamber she does not control and a Senate that blocked one-chamber wins.
summary.grade_rationale: The record shows high ambition and documented action (sponsored bills, votes, petitions, House passage on several items), but most high-magnitude promises stalled at D1-D3 because enactment depended on a Senate and external actors not aligned with her; only background checks, the $15 wage, and the Equality Act reached one-chamber passage, none became law. Delivery is concentrated in advocacy/introduction milestones rather than enacted outcomes, with her causal role frequently shared or supporting.

data_gaps: Most promises lack a campaign-stated deadline, so time_pressure is null throughout. Several delivery buckets rely on her own official-site issue pages (Tier 1) plus secondary news; the domestic-abuser firearm and Equality Act items have thinner corroboration (LIMITED EVIDENCE). As a single House member in a chamber/Senate she does not control, her_role anchors are mostly 0.4-0.6 except where she is the defining sponsor (GND, 0.8). Excluded as non-verifiable/values-only: c10 (guarantee access to reproductive healthcare — directional value), and abolish-ICE (c35/c72 — campaign-era but appears in secondary framing without a clean attributable forward-looking commitment in provided campaign sources) and reproductive-healthcare access framed as a value statement.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 93.3% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
